title: 3 2 1 On the power of everyday conduct, how to find your purpose, and unexpected forms of generosity
author: James Clear
summary: 3-2-1: On the power of everyday conduct, how to find your purpose, and unexpected forms of generosity “The most wisdom per word of any newsletter on the web.” 3-2-1: On the power of everyday conduct, how to find your purpose, and unexpected forms of generosity Happy 3-2-1 Thursday! Here are 3 ide…
fullHtml: 3-2-1: On the power of everyday conduct, how to find your purpose, and unexpected forms of generosity “The most wisdom per word of any newsletter on the web.” 3-2-1: On the power of everyday conduct, how to find your purpose, and unexpected forms of generosity Happy 3-2-1 Thursday! Here are 3 ideas, 2 quotes, and 1 question to consider this week... 3 Ideas From Me read on JAMESCLEAR.COM|MARCH 19, 2026 "The mindset: Confident enough to start. Humble enough to keep learning. The trap: Too humble to start. Or too confident to keep learning." "Unexpected forms of generosity: Being early can be a form of generosity. You wait, so they don't have to. Leaving something unsaid can be a form of generosity. You don't always need the last word. Delivering your work on time can be a form of generosity. You make life easier for everyone downstream. Not taking things personally can be a form of generosity. You give people the space to say things imperfectly." III. "It's rare to find your purpose for life, so instead look for your purpose for this season. What lights you up right now? What's a good thing to dedicate this season of your life to? Perhaps more importantly, what purpose served you well in your previous season, but you have outgrown? Life is always changing. It's okay to pick a new North Star." 2 Quotes From Others Novelist Zadie Smith on the thrill of reading a great book: "The ideal reader cannot sleep when holding the writer he was meant to be with." Source: The Best American Nonrequired Reading Musician Nick Cave on the power of your everyday conduct: "Rather than feel impotent and useless, you must come to terms with the fact that as a human being you are infinitely powerful, and take responsibility for this tremendous power. Even our smallest actions have potential for great change, positively or negatively, and the way in which we all conduct ourselves within the world means something. You are anything but impotent, you are, in fact, exquisitely and frighteningly dynamic, as are we all, and with all respect you have an obligation to stand up and take responsibility for that potential. It is your most ordinary and urgent duty." Source: The Red Hand Files, Issue #216 1 Question For You Always work on the limiting factor. What is holding you back right now? title: Velocidade, custo ou estética... escolha seu lado 🥊
author: Desconhecido
summary: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. Velocidade, custo ou estética... escolha seu lado 壘 Midjourney testa o V8 enquanto o mercado se divide entre rapidez, eficiência e linguagem visual HUMAN MAR 19 LEIA NO APP “Eu estou sempre fazendo aquilo que não sou capaz, numa tentativa…
fullHtml: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. Velocidade, custo ou estética... escolha seu lado 壘 Midjourney testa o V8 enquanto o mercado se divide entre rapidez, eficiência e linguagem visual HUMAN MAR 19 LEIA NO APP “Eu estou sempre fazendo aquilo que não sou capaz, numa tentativa de aprender como fazê-lo.” Vincent Van Gogh Aprender criando sempre fez parte do processo e, com IA, isso fica ainda mais evidente. Novos modelos chegam, ferramentas evoluem e, no meio disso, quem testa, erra e ajusta sai na frente. Reunimos os sinais que ajudam a entender esse momento. Sem muito aviso, o Midjourney abriu para a comunidade o acesso ao V8 em versão alpha e, sendo um dos modelos favoritos de muitos criadores, é claro que a galera correu pra testar. Midjourney libera V8 para testes antes do lançamento oficial A nova versão promete avanços importantes: maior precisão na interpretação de prompts, imagens mais coerentes, melhor renderização de texto e uma velocidade de geração até 5x maior. Também entram novidades como modo HD em 2K nativo, novos controles de estilo e maior integração com moodboards e referências. Mas, nos primeiros testes, o que mais chamou atenção não foi exatamente a lista de melhorias. O V8 reforça o grande diferencial do Midjourney: uma estética própria. As imagens aparecem mais cinematográficas, mais realistas, com luz e cor mais refinadas. A sensação é de um modelo que não só executa melhor, mas começa a interpretar direção visual com mais consistência. O Midjourney sempre ocupou um lugar próprio no ecossistema de IA criativa. Mesmo com o avanço de outros modelos, ele nunca deixou de ser uma das principais escolhas de quem busca linguagem visual, estilo e uma estética mais autoral. O V8 reforça esse posicionamento em um momento em que o mercado começa a se dividir. Enquanto algumas ferramentas avançam em velocidade, custo ou integração, o Midjourney segue aprofundando o que já era seu diferencial: sensibilidade visual e controle estético. Se esse avanço se sustentar fora do ambiente de teste, a mudança vai além da qualidade e passa pelo papel que a ferramenta ocupa dentro do processo criativo. E, considerando o histórico do Midjourney, é exatamente esse tipo de movimento que mantém ele no topo há tanto tempo. Por que isso importa? Brasil será o primeiro país a sediar o Ano da Criatividade A World Creativity Organization declarou 2026 como o Ano da Criatividade no Brasil, após o país ser eleito Creative Country of the Year em Cannes Lions 2025. A iniciativa conecta cultura, negócios e inovação para transformar criatividade em estratégia de desenvolvimento. A nova funcionalidade de Magic Layers transforma imagens estáticas em arquivos editáveis com camadas, recuperando texto, elementos e estrutura do layout. Na prática, permite reabrir uma imagem “finalizada” como se fosse um arquivo de design, sem perder a lógica da composição. O CEO Ted Sarandos disse que IA só faz sentido se deixar filmes e séries melhores, não apenas mais baratos. A Netflix já comprou uma startup de edição com IA e vê a tecnologia como ferramenta criativa, mas reforça: performance humana ainda é insubstituível. O Olaf foi treinado inteiramente em ambiente virtual antes de dar seus primeiros passos nos parques. A NVIDIA mostrou o processo que combina simulação, IA e direção de animação para garantir que o movimento preserve a personalidade do personagem. Canva entra para o universo da edição de imagens geradas por IA Netflix apoia o uso de IA na produção, mas prioriza qualidade Disney leva o processo de animação para o mundo físico com IA A nova versão usa IA generativa junto com dados 3Dpara “prever” partes da imagem, reduzindo processamento e aumentando realismo em jogos. A Nvidia vê esse modelo híbrido como base para outras aplicações além dos games. Veja o comparativo aqui. A Runway apresentou o Characters, API que cria avatares conversacionais em vídeo a partir de uma única imagem. Com voz, expressão e personalidade em tempo real, a proposta é sair do chat e transformar interação com IA em experiência visual. A nova versão do modelo traz avanços em detalhe, movimento e áudio, além de interpretar melhor prompts mais complexos e gerar vídeos já no formato vertical. A ideia é reduzir erros comuns como cenas travadas, movimentos artificiais e inconsistências entre frames. A Human se tornou uma plataforma 360º, centralizando em um só lugar cursos, workflows, ferramentas, comunidade e a Aura, a IA que acompanha seu aprendizado. Tudo conectado pra te ajudar a sair da ideia e chegar na entrega final. Nvidia aposta em IA para criar gráficos em tempo real com DLSS Runway lança personagens interativos em vídeo LTX-2.3 chega com melhora na geração de vídeo e amplia controle Agora somos uma plataforma completa pra você aprender a criar com IA  Uma metologia direta ao ponto, com aplicação prática e focada no que realmente está sendo usado no mercado. Além disso, aulas ao vivo exclusivas para alunos, abrindo um espaço contínuo de colaboração. Com 97% de satisfação, mais de 20.000 criativos já passaram por aqui e hoje fazem parte da comunidade, um espaço pra evoluir com gente de verdade, com troca com outros creators, desafios criativos, vagas e oportunidades reais. Aprenda no seu tempo, entenda processo e construa repertório pra criar melhor com IA. Faça parte da maior plataforma de IA para criativos. Conhecer a plataforma  A série de imagens “Forward”, de @giz.akdag, mostra quão longe a estética cinematográfica com IA pode chegar.  A Bruna Dias, da @diasdecacho, conta como usou personagens criados com IA pra falar de cabelo de um jeito diferente e saiu de 50 mil para 110 mil seguidores.  Claude passa a gerar gráficos e diagramas interativos dentro do chat, ajustando as visualizações conforme a conversa evolui.  Uma banda criada com IA bateu 80 mil ouvintes e depois virou uma banda real, com músicos tocando ao vivo no Japão. 里 O Flora lançou updates que agilizam o workflow, como conectar vários nodes de uma vez e separar variações sem custo extra.  Um criador pegou uma imagem antiga do Midjourney e transformou em um filme, combinando Nano Banana e Kling no mesmo fluxo.  BOAS IDEIAS MERECEM SER VISTAS Toda quinta, sem falta: uma curadoria feita por humanos sobre as novidades de IA e do mercado criativo na sua caixa de entrada  Inscrito → Midjourney V8 Alpha: o que a nova versão em testes promete mudar na geração de imagens com IA Apesar de ainda estar em fase de coleta de feedback, as primeiras gerações já apontam a direção da evolução da plataforma. Como criar Moodboards para imagem com IA: guia para Midjourney, Higgsfield e Freepik Veja como combinar referências visuais para controlar estilo, cores e manter consistência visual na geração de imagens com IA. → 3 técnicas para manter um personagem consistente em 20+ cenas geradas com IA Como manter identidade visual em personagens gerados com IA usando imagens âncora, treinamento leve e referências para sequências longas. → Nano Banana 2 vs Nano Banana Pro: comparativo visual, diferenças e quando usar cada modelo Testamos os dois com os mesmos prompts para mostrar diferenças de qualidade, velocidade e qual modelo usar em cada projeto. Estudo de Vídeo criado pela nossa aluna Mariana Teles Ángel Castellanos é um fotógrafo venezuelano que vive em São Paulo. Seu trabalho transita entre retrato e moda e já apareceu em publicações como Vogue e Numéro. O que mais chama atenção é a forma como constrói atmosfera nas imagens, com composições que exploram luz, cor e cidade para criar calor, movimento e uma estética latino- americana contemporânea. Clique aqui para ver a curadoria completa. Vencedor do Oscar, o Diretor Ryan Coogler comenta a construção de um dos seus planos favoritos em Sinners, filme que levou 3 estatuetas, incluindo Melhor Ator pelo Michael B. Jordan. Um lembrete de como direção, timing e atuação continuam sendo o coração da cena. ENQUETE  HUMAN SHOWCASE @mtelesdesigner Mariana Teles on Instagram: "E se um filme publicitário para o ... Indicações que vão expandir o seu horizonte  A estética urbana e sensível de Ángel Castellanos  Dentro de um plano de Sinners @shotdeck SHOTDECK on Instagram: "Everyone O... O que achou dessa edição? Amei  Gostei  Achei ok  Compartilhe com colegas que também querem entender o que está rolando em IA e no mercado criativo. title: 5 tópicos de Terça #76
author: 5 Tópicos de Terça
summary: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. 5 tópicos de Terça #76 Mensalidade vs Anuidade, preços de IA, questionamentos, tweets e quotes LUCAS BITTENCOURT AND EDUARDO BELOTTI MAR 17 LEIA NO APP Toda terça-feira, 5 ideias na sua caixa de entrada com a curadoria especial de seus fo…
fullHtml: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. 5 tópicos de Terça #76 Mensalidade vs Anuidade, preços de IA, questionamentos, tweets e quotes LUCAS BITTENCOURT AND EDUARDO BELOTTI MAR 17 LEIA NO APP Toda terça-feira, 5 ideias na sua caixa de entrada com a curadoria especial de seus founders favoritos (dica: Lucas e Edu). “Eu ouço e esqueço. Eu vejo e lembro. Eu faço e entendo.” - Confucius “We protect money because it’s visible and throw away time because it’s not.” - Unknown “To get what you want, deserve what you want. Trust, success, and admiration are earned.” - Charlie Munger 異 1. Quotes da semana 異 2. Diferença entre cobrar anual vs mensal Existe um motivo pelo qual as empresas de software dão até 30% de desconto para quem fecha o plano anual: É MUITO MELHOR PARA ELAS. Parece que o ganho é só no curto prazo, enquanto se abre mão de dinheiro no longo prazo, mas isso não é verdade. Em uma empresa que tem crescimento contínuo, cobrar anuidade resulta em mais dinheiro no longo prazo TAMBÉM. Parece meio esquisito e é por isso que eu montei uma calculadora para você conseguir ver e usar esses insights para melhorar o seu negócio. Dá uma brincada. Mexe na mensalidade, no desconto da anuidade, no churn, no crescimento... e repare nos gráficos. A anuidade só começa a não valer muito a pena se o desconto for maior do que ~40%. Confere aqui: saas-calc.offthegrid.com.br Lá para 2017, rolou uma competição feroz entre Cabify, 99 e Uber. Cada um dava cupons melhores que o outro para descontos em viagens. Eu voltava para casa TODOS os dias de carro de aplicativo, mesmo que não precisasse porque tava barato demais. E eu também não tinha nenhuma lealdade. Um dia era de Uber, no outro de Cabify e no outro de 99. Era 100% baseado em desconto. Hoje, em 2026, estamos vivendo a MESMA situação, mas com AI. Essas assinaturas de R$100/mês na verdade custam muito mais para a OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc. Eles estão subsidiando para acelerar o uso. Igualzinho 99, Cabify e Uber fizeram. 異 3. Aproveite os preços de AI O CEO da Cursor fez um estudo que mostra que o coding plan de $200 do Claude MAX custa cerca de $5.000 para a companhia. Estamos falando de um “cupom” de ~R$25.000 TODO MÊS. Aproveite esse momento de AI. O preço vai acabar aumentando no futuro e você vai se arrepender. Toma ai alguns links meus que dão algum desconto, mas não se prenda a eles. Assine o que fizer mais sentido para você. Os preços estão muito baixos. Experimente Claude Cowork Experimente Z.ai (Ai Chinesa que compete com Claude por uma fração do preço) Experimente o Clawdio, que funciona basicamente como um mordomo virtual. Você dá tarefas pelo Whatsapp e ele executa. Tem trial gratuito! Experimenta o Clawdio aqui. A pergunta de um milhão de dólares (ou bilhão, ou trilhão?) pairando sobre nossas cabeças nesse exato momento é essa: as pessoas vão “vibe codar” seu próprio software? Eu não tô falando sobre a galera usando o Lovable pra fazer apps fofinhos com o calendário compartilhado da família ou to-do list personalizada. Isso aí beleza, vai rolar cada vez mais e é muito maneiro. Mas o cagaço atual do mercado é se alguém dentro de uma empresa vai usar AI pra criar um software interno de recrutamento em 30 min e parar de usar uma Gupy, por exemplo. 異 4. O average joe vai construir seu próprio SaaS? O risco que software virar commodity causa pras empresas de SaaS ainda é muito difícil de prever. Isso causa incerteza. E o mercado não gosta de incerteza. Inclusive, uma das coisas que os investidores mais gostavam da indústria de SaaS era justamente a pouca incerteza, ou seja, a previsibilidade. Hoje, com qualquer moleque de 15 anos com acesso a ferramentas de vibe coding recriando um SaaS em um fim de semana, voltamos a questão inicial. Quando custa muito pouco construir um clone do Slack, aquela cobrança de meio de milhão de reais anual pro seu time ter um chat interno fica cada vez mais tentadora de ser limada. O que o mercado segue pregando é que nem todo SaaS é igual (e obviamente todo mundo diz que o seu é diferente, hehe). Há quem diga que os System of Records estão melhores posicionados para construir “System of Actions” em cima deles e entregar o trabalho ao invés de ferramentas. Do outro lado tem gente dizendo que os incumbentes todos vão ruir nessa mudança de plataforma. Como sempre, eu acho que a resposta tá no meio termo. Mas voltando a nossa pergunta: será que a Suzana da Contabilidade vai realmente usar seu tempo livre pra vibe codar uma alternativa ao Conta Azul? Acho que não. Assim como hoje em dia você pode criar suas soluções de software, você também pode costurar suas roupas ou construir seus móveis. Isso não quer dizer que você deveria. Sei que o exemplo da Suzana da Contabilidade é extremo, em teoria você só precisa de uma pessoa mais “tech savvy” dentro de uma organização para imitar a Klarna e realizar uma caça as bruxas aos custos de SaaS. Mas de novo, não é só porque você pode, que você deve. Até porque quem já lidou com sistemas legados sabe o tamanho da dor de cabeça que vem com a manutenção e atualização deles. Enfim, como eu já disse, tem muita incerteza envolvida, o mercado ainda tá descobrindo como precificar isso enquanto o chão se move sob nossos pés a cada tweet da Anthropic ou lançamento de modelo chinês. Só sei que vivemos tempos incríveis (e estranhos) e prefiro seguir a máxima de que a melhor maneira de prever o futuro é construi-lo. [conteúdo patrocinado] A revolução da AI no e-commerce não está chegando, ela já chegou. E quem se adaptar mais rápido vai sair na frente. Os e-commerces que usam os agentes de IA da StationZero já estão na vanguarda desse movimento. Esses colaboradores digitais vivem nos canais dos times (Slack, Teams, WhatsApp, Email, etc) e executam tarefas como qualquer analista faria, só que 24/7. Sem precisar de integração complexa ou semanas de projeto. Não é um chatbot. Não é mais uma ferramenta com painel. É trabalho entregue, o tipo de tarefa que hoje ocupa horas do seu time de marketing e operações. Estamos abrindo 5 vagas essa semana pra e-commerces testarem. Se quiser experimentar, se inscreve na lista de espera em stationzero.ai ENQUETE Qual seu tópico favorito da semana? 異 5. Tweet pra descontrair do SaaSpocalipse 異 6. Mais que chatbot, é AI que faz trabalho Quotes Mensal vs Anual Aproveite os preços Average Joe vai fazer SaaS? Leu até aqui e ainda não se inscreveu na newsletter? Não tem problema. Se inscreve agora enquanto nem o Lucas nem o Edu tão olhando e eles não vão ficar bravos com você. title: As 6 Operações Mentais da Análise
author: Desconhecido
summary: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. As 6 Operações Mentais da Análise Frameworks são só marketing HEITOR SASAKI MAR 15 LEIA NO APP Se tu tirasses todos os frameworks da tua cabeça, MECE, 5 Porquês, driver tree, Pareto, tu ainda saberias analisar um problema de negócio? Se a…
fullHtml: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. As 6 Operações Mentais da Análise Frameworks são só marketing HEITOR SASAKI MAR 15 LEIA NO APP Se tu tirasses todos os frameworks da tua cabeça, MECE, 5 Porquês, driver tree, Pareto, tu ainda saberias analisar um problema de negócio? Se a resposta for “não sei”, tu decoraste frameworks sem entender o que eles fazem por baixo. Eu passei anos achando que ser sênior era acumular frameworks. Mais siglas, mais metodologias, mais templates. Até que percebi uma coisa. O controller financeiro que abre o DRE todo mês pra encontrar onde a margem caiu está executando a mesma operação mental que o consultor McKinsey num case de profitability. O engenheiro de produção que identifica o gargalo da linha está fazendo o mesmo que o analista de dados quando mapeia um funil de conversão. Talvez nenhum dos dois ouviram falar de MECE. Mas ainda assim estão analisando corretamente o problema. Frameworks são combinações codificadas de um número pequeno de operações mentais. Quando tu entendes as operações, tu consegues "construir” o framework certo pra cada situação. Inclusive pra situações que nenhum livro cobriu. Temos que entender os princípios. Mapeei 6 operações que aparecem por trás de praticamente todo problema analítico que já enfrentei. Enquadrar é delimitar o problema antes de investigar. “Qual é exatamente a pergunta? Quem usa a resposta, e pra decidir o quê?” É a operação mais importante e a mais pulada. Separar é pegar algo que parece uma coisa e enxergar como múltiplas coisas. A própria palavra “análise” vem do grego analyein, que significa decompor. Receita vem de preço × volume × mix. Um processo tem etapas. Todo framework é, no fundo, uma forma codificada de Separar. Quantificar é atribuir magnitude. Transformar “temos um problema de custo” em “temos um problema de R$2M em logística, que é 40% do aumento total.” Sem dimensionar, tu tratas tudo com o mesmo peso. Comparar é colocar lado a lado. Nenhum número faz sentido sozinho. A informação só surge na comparação: temporal (antes/depois), contra padrão (meta, SLA, benchmark), entre grupos (regiões, produtos, coortes). Conectar é identificar o que causa, depende ou se move junto. Dados mostram que X e Y se movem juntos. Testar hipóteses. Só o processo explica se X causa Y ou se ambos são efeito de Z. Sintetizar é remontar tudo em uma história que leva à ação. Tu desmontaste, dimensionaste, comparaste, conectaste. Agora precisa remontar numa recomendação. Sem isso, tu entregas dados e espera que o gestor monte a história sozinho. Um exemplo de como isso muda na prática. O gestor pede: “Me lista os problemas da área.” O júnior lista 12 problemas. “Todos são importantes.” Nenhum dimensionado. O gestor recebe a lista, não sabe por onde começar e pede pra outra pessoa refazer. O sênior dimensiona. “Dos 12, três explicam 85% do impacto. O maior, atraso na entrega da região Norte, custa R$1.2M por mês. Os outros dois somam R$600K. Recomendo começar pelo Norte.” Mesma informação. Impacto completamente diferente. A diferença está na operação mental, não na ferramenta. E cada tipo de problema de negócio tem uma sequência natural dessas operações que o profissional sênior já faz intuitivamente. E a boa notícia é que os problemas são quase sempre os mesmos. A sequência para abordar esse problema pode mudar, mas operações são as mesmas. Essa semana no Data Creators, apresentei o material com as sequências de aplicação pra 9 tipos de problema clássicos de negócio (churn, receita, pricing, growth, etc), a tabela que mapeia frameworks clássicos como combinações dessas operações, e exemplos de como profissionais de outras áreas já executam cada uma sem saber o nome. Sem falar de um prompt que vai nos ajudar a organizar nossos pensamentos. O melhor uso da IA é quando combinamos com a nossa expertise. O meu objetivo é de mostrar que tu provavelmente já fizeste todas essas operações e que essa atividade não é exclusiva da área de dados. Conhecimento tu já tens, talvez falte uma perspectiva diferente. Heitor Sasaki To com 4 vagas abertas para o DC. Se queres participar, leia o doc e me mande mensagem no Linkedin/insta. Vais ter acesso a todos os nossos materiais + os prompts que temos (escrita, entrevista, currículo, negócios e o DVP) (podes ver os resultados do DC no Insta) Quero ser um DC (clique) Data Creators Siga nas redes: Instagram (Updates do Data Creator, Caixinha de Perguntas) COMPARTILHAR CURTIRCOMENTARRESTACK © 2026 Heitor Sasaki 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 Cancelar inscrição

summary: 4 Minute Fridays: The Most Anti-Inflammatory Nutrient On The Planet, The 3 Hour Rule That Fixes Your Blood Sugar A... 4 Minute Fridays: The Most Anti- Inflammatory Nutrient On The Planet, The 3 Hour Rule That Fixes Your Blood Sugar And Heart Health Overnight & The Creatine I Use Every This articl…
fullHtml: 4 Minute Fridays: The Most Anti-Inflammatory Nutrient On The Planet, The 3 Hour Rule That Fixes Your Blood Sugar A... 4 Minute Fridays: The Most Anti- Inflammatory Nutrient On The Planet, The 3 Hour Rule That Fixes Your Blood Sugar And Heart Health Overnight & The Creatine I Use Every This article first appeared in my private newsletter on March 6, DAN GO MAR 13 READ IN APP First Posted on Dango.co Welcome to 4 Minute Fridays, my weekly newsletter, where I reveal cutting-edge tips to help you get lean, boost energy, and live longer. The most anti-inflammatory nutrient on the planet The 3 Hour Rule that fixes your blood sugar and heart health overnight The creatine I use every day What’s In Store For Today: Hey friend, An old donkey fell into a deep well. The farmer figured it wasn’t worth saving. He called his neighbors to shovel dirt in and bury it. But something unexpected happened. Every time dirt hit the donkey’s back, it shook it off and stepped up. Load after load, it kept rising. Layer by layer, the thing that was meant to bury it became the platform it stood on. Eventually, the donkey walked right out of the well. Most people treat problems the way the farmer expected the donkey to react. They let the weight pile up until it buries them. The ones who win do something different. They take what’s thrown at them, shake it off, and use it to climb higher. Here’s what caught my attention this week... Most people obsess over cold plunges and red light therapy to slow aging. But a study of 5,674 adults found one of the strongest anti-aging signals hiding in the most boring place: dietary fiber. Researchers measured telomere length (a biological marker of how fast your cells are aging) against fiber intake. The results were striking. Every 10g increase in fiber per 1,000 calories was linked to telomeres that were 67 base pairs longer. Translated into real terms, that’s roughly 4-5 fewer years of biological aging. Here Is Your 4 Minute Friday: 1. The Most Anti-Inflammatory Nutrient On The Planet This is important because the average American eats less than half the recommended fiber intake. The cells are aging faster because you’re not eating enough plants, legumes, and whole grains. Fiber feeds your gut bacteria, reduces inflammation, and apparently, keeps your DNA younger, too. You don’t need a longevity clinic for this one. You just need more vegetables on your plate. One of the best things I’ve ever done for my body was stopping eating three hours before bed, and for good reason. Researchers split 39 overweight adults into two groups. One group just stopped eating 3 hours before bed. After 7.5 weeks, the early cutoff group had measurably better heart rate variability, lower nighttime heart rate, lower cortisol at night, and improved blood sugar response the next morning. Their blood pressure also dipped more overnight, which is a key marker of cardiovascular health that most people never think about. When you eat close to bed, your body is still digesting and managing blood sugar instead of recovering and regulating itself. Giving it a 3- hour runway helps it do its job. Next time you’re at home, set a kitchen closing time. If you sleep at 11pm, stop eating at 8pm. That’s it. It sounds simple, but it may be the best thing you do for your body in a long time. 2. The 3 Hour Rule That Fixes Your Blood Sugar And Heart Health Overnight As you know, I’m a huge fan of creatine. It’s the supplement I recommend to most of my clients looking to improve body composition, maximize strength, increase daily energy, and even boost cognition. The brand I take is Create. They popularized creatine gummies and are all over the place (Joe Rogan, Kim Kardashian, and Adam Levine are big fans). I’ve talked in the past about how I prefer their powders over the gummies, but they just introduced a sour cherry flavor, and I must admit they did a great job! All products use Creapure Creatine Monohydrate, the best form of creatine on the planet, and it’s tested by third parties like Eurofins for potency and safety. The best part? I know the founders, and that means all subscribers of my newsletter get 30% off. Use code DANGO30 to get 30% off. Arnold came into the program wanting to lose weight, improve his energy, and build consistency with his health. He’s now down 36 lbs, lost 10.38 inches off his waist, and is seeing muscle definition for the first time. Along the way, he eliminated late- night snacking and now feels a noticeable boost in his daily energy. Even with a busy schedule and frequent travel, Arnold has stayed consistent with his workouts, making the most of whatever equipment is available. ❤️ My Favourite Thing This Week Client Of The Week - Arnold, Founder Here’s how we did it: Focused on strength training and simple, effective workouts Eliminated late-night snacking by building structured eating habits Created a routine that works anywhere, even while traveling Arnold didn’t just lose weight, he built a lifestyle that keeps him moving forward. Amazing work, Arnold! Are you an entrepreneur who wants to drop the fat, gain muscle, and boost energy? Click this link to apply for our coaching program. “If you feel like you’re losing everything, remember, trees lose their leaves every year, yet they still stand tall and wait for better days to come.” ― Japanese saying When life gets overwhelming, I just remind myself of three things: 1. This too shall pass. 2. This is a season. The last one: All this is happening to make me a stronger person. As promised, get healthier in under 4 minutes. - Dan Tucker LA. Dietary Fiber and Telomere Length in 5674 U.S. Adults: An NHANES Study of Biological Aging. Nutrients. 2018 Mar 23;10(4):400. doi: 10.3390/nu10040400. PMID: 29570620; PMCID: PMC5946185. Sleep-Aligned Extended Overnight Fasting Improves Nighttime and Daytime Cardiometabolic FunctionDaniela Grimaldi Sabra M. One Quote To Finish Your Week Strong References Abbott Kristen L. Knutson and Phyllis C. Zee p- Disclaimer: This email is provided for educational and informational purposes only and does not constitute providing medical advice or professional services. The information provided should not be used for diagnosing or treating a health problem or disease, and those seeking personal medical advice should consult with a licensed physician. title: 3 2 1 The price of compromise, the magic of art, and how to improve in the modern world
author: James Clear
summary: 3-2-1: The price of compromise, the magic of art, and how to improve in the modern world “The most wisdom per word of any newsletter on the web.” 3-2-1: The price of compromise, the magic of art, and how to improve in the modern world Happy 3-2-1 Thursday! Here are 3 ideas, 2 quotes, and 1 questi…
fullHtml: 3-2-1: The price of compromise, the magic of art, and how to improve in the modern world “The most wisdom per word of any newsletter on the web.” 3-2-1: The price of compromise, the magic of art, and how to improve in the modern world Happy 3-2-1 Thursday! Here are 3 ideas, 2 quotes, and 1 question to consider this week... 3 Ideas From Me "You are not your grand plans. You are your daily patterns." read on JAMESCLEAR.COM|MARCH 12, 2026 "Questions are answered as you move, not before you move. Moving forward with an answer that is partially correct will usually fill in the gaps faster than waiting until you come up with a plan that is perfectly correct." III. "The modern world is optimized for convenience, not improvement. The default path is usually the more convenient path. And I get it. Who wants their days to be a pain in the neck? I like sitting in air- conditioned rooms and watching my favorite shows too. But the body and mind only grow when placed under a stimulus. If you want improvement, you have to choose something different than convenience. It can be lovely to have a day where you do not push yourself, but it rarely works out well if you have a life where you do not push yourself." 2 Quotes From Others Painter and printmaker Mary Cassatt reminds us to stand tall and be our full selves: "Acceptance, under someone else's terms, is worse than rejection." Source: Mary Cassatt: Painting the Modern Woman Abstract painter Agnes Martin reminds us that art can deliver those rare, fleeting experiences of clarity, inner stillness, and harmony: "The function of art is the renewal of memories of moments of perfection." Source: "On the Perfection Underlying Life" (1973 lecture at the Institute of Contemporary Art) 1 Question For You How much of your ambition is borrowed from other people's desires? Want to share this issue of 3-2-1? Just copy and paste this link: Until next week, James Clear Author of Atomic Habits Cofounder of Authors Equity p.s. How to exercise while you travel. What else am I working on? Get my book Read my #1 New York Times bestseller, Atomic Habits, which has sold more than 25 million copies worldwide. The Atomic Habits Workbook The official companion to Atomic Habits. Filled with simple exercises for building the life you want. My book recommendations this week In addition to my own writing, I cofounded a publishing company called Authors Equity to help other authors publish their books. As a result, I get a front row seat to a lot of great books. Here are a few books I think you'll enjoy: OVER 1 MILLION COPIES SOLD The Simple Path to Wealth Your road map to financial independence and a rich, free life by JL Collins Print.Audio.Ebook OVER 1 MILLION COPIES SOLD Almanack of Naval Ravikant How to get rich without getting lucky by Eric Jorgenson Print.Audio. Ebook OVER 1 MILLION COPIES SOLD The 1-Page Marketing Plan Get new customers, make more money, and stand out from the crowd by Allan Dib Print .Audio. Ebook About this newsletter: You are receiving this email because you subscribed to my weekly 3-2-1 newsletter. Every Thursday, I share 3 ideas from me, 2 quotes from others, and 1 question for you to ponder. Occasionally, I send out longer content on habits and self- improvement. subscriber profile. bonuses. This will also remove you from any promotional emails for the book. 2935 E Main St. Unit #9361, Columbus, OH 43209

title: How to start a one person business in 2026 (things changed)
author: Desconhecido
summary: How to start a one-person business in 2026 (things changed) What does a $1m one-person business look like now that everyone has AI? DAN KOE MAR 11 READ IN APP How do you build a one-person business in 2026? Well, you do everything you would have done before, but you use AI to do it faster, with h…
fullHtml:
How to start a one-person business in

2026 (things changed) What does a $1m one-person business look like now that everyone has AI? DAN KOE MAR 11 READ IN APP How do you build a one-person business in 2026? Well, you do everything you would have done before, but you use AI to do it faster, with higher quality, and with less guesswork. You still generate traffic. You still write content. You still write emails/newsletters. You still create a product or service. You still create a customer avatar. You still formulate a compelling offer. You still build a landing page. You still write persuasive copy for that page. You still put the offer in front of people and see if they buy. But that’s a lot. Very few will do it. Before AI was such a big thing, social media was the thing that allowed one-person to build what we call a “lifestyle business.” But of course, now that the barrier of entry is lower, anyone can try to do this, meaning there is more “competition,” but most people barely try, and they especially don’t improve and iterate, and when things get rough they try to just have AI do it all for them. So, the barrier of entry is lower, but the skill cap is higher. If you take what you learn here, I feel like you’ll have a solid starting point. For the sake of this letter, we’re going to set the goal of making $1 million as one person just so we can make this tangible. I’m going to share exactly what you need to learn, how to use AI better than everyone else, and prompts that you can use today to start your business in about a week. Note: We are not going to be using crazy agent workflows in this letter, but we may in the future if you want them. We are going over the fundamentals. The fact of the matter is that most people using things like OpenClaw and Claude Code are wasting their time and money. They just get a dopamine hit from watching an agent do stuff and thinking they’re “doing 10 peoples worth of work” yet they have nothing to show for it. I’m not saying agents aren’t useful, I’m saying people don’t understand what they’re doing. They think an agent absolved them from learning. Before we start, let’s break down a few realistic paths to get to $1 million a year as one-person. ￼ You need to at least believe this is possible before you do it. ￼ $1,000,000 / 12 = $83,333 per month $83,333 / 30 = $2,777 per day How To Make $1 Million As One Person, Without The Gimmicks Now, there are a few ways to do that. Sell 18 $150 products a day (i.e. a course) Sell 111 $25 subscriptions a day (i.e. Substack) Land one $5000 client every other day (i.e. freelancing) Land one $10,000 client every 4 days (i.e. coaching / consulting) A combination of both. 1-2 clients a week and a few product or subscription sales a day. Or whatever other combination and price point there is to hit $1 million. If you go the client route, you will either need a team at some point or be good enough to orchestrate AI/agents to fulfill on the service. One person can only do so much client work, but it can be done. Either way, going the client route is a good option for beginners - because you don’t have an audience yet and can focus on reaching out to people individually. As a beginner, it’s much easier to sell a $1000-$5000 service to one person than it is to sell 100-200 Substack subscriptions or courses. If you go the product or subscription route, like a book, course, niche vibe coded software, or membership community, you will need a lot of traffic. You probably aren’t going to go viral randomly when nobody knows about you. How do you get enough traffic to make that many product or service sales? At a 2.5% conversion rate on a landing page that allows people to purchase your product, you need 720 people to visit that page a day to make 18 sales on a $150 product. Or you need a post to go viral once or twice a month, resulting in huge spikes in traffic, but I wouldn’t bet on that, because virality usually doesn’t result in a lot of sales. Where do those 720 people come from? Social media, ads, SEO, or some other form of influencer marketing or word of mouth. As a beginner with little money to spend on testing ads until they work, social media is probably your best option. It’s free and building an audience is leverage. So, if we assume you become skilled at social media (yes, social media is a skill, with a little bit of luck involved), you can: Get 10-50,000 views per YouTube video Get 500k to 1 million impressions on social media per month Yes, it will take some time to do that, maybe a year of non-distracted execution, but it’s possible, and we’re taking on the big goal of $1 million a year. Many people would be happy with a tiny fraction of that. That means out of that many people, you need only 720 people a day to click on your landing page in order to make $1 million a year. That’s pretty doable. For those who are used to time and labor as their source of generating money, it is a bit “out there” to think it can be that simple. That’s what your employer does to make multiples on top of what they pay you to execute one specific part of the process that makes them money. But of course, it’s simple not easy yada yada I get it. It can take 2-5 years to get really good at offer creation, social media marketing, and copywriting. But if we “train” AI on the principles of them all, and feed them data from legendary marketers, you’d be shocked how well AI can do those things for you. Upgrade to paid The core difference between building a one-person business this year vs a few years ago is this: You have AI. Not to do everything for you, but to help you learn, execute, and iterate faster. An entire course can be a prompt. You can chat with your favorite guru’s content to get “coaching” from them How To Build A One-Person Business With AI, The Entire Workflow without paying them. You don’t have to scour the internet for niche information anymore. That’s incredible. This is the template we are going to go over. I’d highly recommend using it and working through it on a desktop. Save the link as a reminder in your phone if you have to. There’s also a folder with all the files in the canvas here. If you follow this exactly, and use the provided templates, you will have the 3 pillars of a modern, successful business. At minimum you will have a great starting point. Brand – Who you are, what you help people achieve, and why people should care about both. Content – Your ideas, opinions, and teachings that attract people to your brand. Offer – Your product, service, and compelling landing page you can send people to from your content. Of course, no matter how smart AI is, all of this stuff is variable. Running through this once isn’t going to instantly make you successful. You will need to notice what doesn’t work, try something else, and continue repeating until you see results. And even then, you’ll need to repeat the process because once something works, it eventually stops working. Thanks, human nature making novelty normalize. That said, I’ll also provide a way to do that with AI, so you don’t get stuck. As one person, on social media, you are starting a personal brand. Why? Because if you want to make money, you need something to sell and a way to get people to see it. A personal brand is neither of those things, but you can think of it like a digital storefront (or resume) that adds a layer of trust - trust is a moat in the age of AI. Your offer is what you sell, your content is how you get people to it, your brand is the umbrella for both. 1) Personal Brand Strategy Oh, and I don’t care how cringe you think the word personal brand is. That’s what you’re doing if you are posting as a person on social media. If it helps, just think of it as a vessel for meaningful work. We’ve discussed this plenty of times before in past letters and videos, so I’m going to be brief. This is my recommended strategy, not the only strategy, for social media. You are the niche. Your beliefs, experiences, and interests give you a unique point of view that reflects in your content and products. You need a few content pillars: one skill or interest you plan to monetize, and two complementary interests that you can’t shut up about. You need to ground those content pillars in pain points, foundational content topics, and high-performing ideas. You need to turn all of that into a 1-2 sentence social media bio that gets across the most attractive parts. From there, everything is easier to do. It’s easier to generate product and service ideas, create a content calendar or cadence, and more. How do you use AI to make this process easier? When it comes to personal brand strategy, there are a few ways you can use AI (in a better way than just asking questions to a chat app). First is curating expert information and having a conversation with it. As an example, you could find YouTube videos or books from people who are certified experts and add those to a chat rather than asking for a general AI’s opinion. AI works best when you know what you want. That’s the simplest one. Second is turning those sources of information into a prompt that interviews you and generates a strategy that’s tailored to you. Both of those are what I’ve included on the canvas above. You can duplicate it and start chatting with it here (use on desktop, save the link for later if you need to). Third is doing more complex things that people may not need. Creating a personal brand coach agent that has context of your brand, researches competitor content, and suggests content ideas is one example. I can go into this in a future letter, just like this or let me know and I can. I’ve been doing this a while. I can read a tweet, newsletter, or YouTube title and know whether or not it’s going to do well. The biggest problem beginner’s make is not illustrating the importance of the idea they are trying to convey. They have interesting ideas, but they can’t make it interesting to other people. In order to make an idea interesting to someone else, you need to provide a compelling “why.” You need to give them a reason to change their behavior. Because if you’re the one who changes their behavior, they will remember you as the person who “changed their life,” and they will start to trust you over any human or AI. In other words, the secret to writing content is to (1) have a good idea and (2) have a pain point it solves or a benefit it gives, and you need to illustrate that reason well. The best way to understand this is to read good ideas and look for the pain point they’re solving or benefit they are promising.** The second mistake they make is not understanding how things fit together. They are good at writing content, but they feel like it’s pointless because they don’t know how to make an income from it, so they start catering to the algorithm by posting feel good nonsense hoping that the platform monetization system will pay them enough to eventually quit their job. As someone with a few million followers, I can tell you that platform monetization is not enough. I can make about 2) How To Start Writing Better-Than-Most Content Immediately 300-1000 a month on Instagram or X, but with my own product/offer, I can make 50k-200k+. Now, since this isn’t an entire course on content, I’m going to give you the 80/20 of what to do: Write down ideas like crazy – Read more books, listen to podcasts, reflect on your life and try to “catch” ideas that are unique and beneficial Immediately think of a pain point or benefit – Train your mind to think of why this idea is important to more than just yourself Save ideas that you like the structure of – Have a folder in Eden (you can paste direct social links and reference the folder with AI if you want) where you save ideas. As a beginner, imitate the structure or framework of these with your own ideas as the topic. Then, simply practice writing until it becomes second nature. Now, when it comes to where you should post, I recommend having a content ecosystem or content waterfall system. It looks like this: I write one newsletter a week – This is on a topic that aligns with my content pillars from the brand strategy we went over earlier. That newsletter becomes a soft script for one YouTube video a week, that YouTube video get’s posted to podcast platforms. For posts, I can pull ideas from that week’s newsletter (using a prompt we go over below), or I can just write posts as normal by generating my own ideas. My best posts get used as a soft Reels/YT shorts script – I just read over the ideas and then start speaking to the camera. Since they are already validated they tend to do better than if I were going about it blindly. My other posts get turned into carousels for LinkedIn, Instagram, and the YouTube community page. As one person, I believe it’s better to focus on a few quality pieces of content (one long form a week and a few short form a day) and then spread those to all platforms. Trying to create unique content simply decreases the quality of each idea. Okay, now how do you use AI to make this process easier? Since this isn’t an entire content guide, I can’t really teach you the ins and outs of post or newsletter writing (because that would be very long - I could write a book on it). What I can do, however, is give you prompts that I’ve personally written that contain my way of thinking. If you simply run through the prompts, you will learn more about post and newsletter writing in 30 minutes than you would with months of trial and error. Inside the canvas template that you can duplicate, you can read the sticky notes and follow the instructions. One piece of advice: do not post what the AI generates. Use the post and newsletter generations as first drafts or prototypes. I would highly recommend reading through each one and refining them according to how you would say it. So far you have a few pieces of the one-person business equation. You have a brand that illustrates your values and attracts the right kind of people. You have content that slowly builds trust over time and allows you to reach more people. Simply put, you have people whose trust grows in you over time. Most businesses could only wish for that. Too many people build a great business but they can’t attract customers for the life of them, and if they don’t have customers, they will not earn a penny. 3) What You Sell Now, this section makes me kind of mad, because I spent 5 years of my life studying and practicing marketing, sales, offer creation, copywriting, and more. In today’s world, you can have AI do most of this for you if you know how to “train” the AI to use frameworks and knowledge from great marketers, salesman, and copywriters. That’s what I’ve done for you, but it helps to know what you should do here: Create a detailed customer avatar – This is something you’ll keep safe and reference often whenever you are creating marketing materials. Create your first offer – Using your customer avatar and offer creation principles, create a product or service that they can’t resist. Turn both into a compelling landing page draft – Most people create a boring product and then just illustrate what the product does on their landing page and wonder why nobody wants it. When you go through these prompts, they will first interview you. Answer all of the questions as throughly as you can. They will spit out a full customer avatar document, a full offer blueprint, and full landing page copy. Simply follow the order that they are in and you should be good to go. A final note: I can’t guarantee that the generations will be 100% accurate or work perfectly well. AI, frankly, isn’t good enough on its own. The person directing it is where the magic lies. You have everyone spending thousands a month on agents that do everything for them, yet their lives haven’t changed that much and they aren’t instantly successful. Most people are quite literally just burning money to watch a computer produce something mediocre because they don’t know what good looks like. You will still have to learn. You will still have to practice. And most importantly, you will still have to iterate when something doesn’t work until it does work. I hope this was helpful for you, and I hope you learn some useful skills as you go through the prompts provided here. I know it’s been a while since I last sent a letter out, but I should be back in the groove of things now. So i’ll see you in a week or so. – Dan LIKECOMMENTRESTACK © 2026 DAN KOE 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104

title: If You Want To Lose 75% Of Your Visceral Fat In 90 Days, Copy This
author: Desconhecido
summary: If You Want To Lose 75% Of Your Visceral Fat In 90 Days, Copy This: This article first appeared in my private newsletter on March 3, DAN GO MAR 10 READ IN APP First Posted on Dango.co Mike sat across from me on the Zoom call in frustration. “Dan, I’m working out 6 times a week. I’m losing weight.…
fullHtml:
If You Want To Lose 75% Of Your

Visceral Fat In 90 Days, Copy

This:

This article first appeared in my private newsletter on March 3, DAN GO MAR 10 READ IN APP First Posted on Dango.co Mike sat across from me on the Zoom call in frustration. “Dan, I’m working out 6 times a week. I’m losing weight. I’m doing everything right, but the visceral fat is not budging”, he told me. Mike was successful by most accounts. Great business. Amazing family man. He was crushing it in every area of life except his health. He had over 30 pounds to lose, but worse than that, he had 3+ pounds of visceral fat he couldn’t shake. I had to give him a reality check. “Losing weight is not a guarantee that you’re going to lose visceral fat. It’s more complicated than that. If you want to get rid of this fat, you need to get serious and make some lifestyle changes. It’s not just about calories in, calories out. If you are ready for that, we can lose it in 90 days flat”, I replied. “90 days? You serious” “Does a bear s*** in the woods?” “I’m ready. Sign me up”. 90 days later, his weight is down 32 pounds, and we eliminated 75% of his visceral fat. In today’s newsletter, I want to share with you the protocol we used with clients (and Mike) to eliminate this dangerous fat from your body in 90 days. Visceral Fat (VAT) wraps around your organs. Unlike the fat you can pinch, it’s metabolically active. It releases inflammatory compounds directly into your bloodstream. These compounds drive chronic inflammation, which is linked to heart disease, diabetes, Alzheimer’s, and certain cancers. The crazy part? You can lose weight and not lose visceral fat. Visceral fat doesn’t respond to calories alone. It responds to hormonal and inflammatory signals. When you just cut calories without changing food quality, you’re still allowing your organs, gut, and insulin to live in the inflamed environment that contributes to visceral fat. This is why so many people do everything “right” by conventional standards and still carry visceral fat. They may be in a calorie deficit, but they’re not addressing the root causes: inflammation, gut health, stress hormones, and sleep. Visceral fat is not about math, it’s about systems. Here’s how to get rid of it: Why Visceral Fat Is Different Traditional Mediterranean Diets focus on fatty fish and olive oil, while the Optimized Mediterranean Diet focuses on polyphenol intake, increased protein, and time-restricted feeding. From my experience, this has been the most effective diet for reducing visceral fat while building muscle. First, we start with ELIMINATION: Eliminated Alcohol: Mike was a one to two glasses of wine per day guy. Alcohol increases inflammation, spikes appetite, and your body prioritizes burning it before anything else. We didn’t say forever. Just for 90 days. Cut Processed Foods: Anything packaged, fast food, or hyperprocessed. The emulsifiers and added sugars wreck your gut, which directly impacts visceral fat storage. This isn’t about restriction. It’s about creating an internal environment where visceral fat can’t survive. Then we work on ADDITION: Increased Protein: We got Mike to 0.7 to 1 gram per pound of body weight from real food sources. Fish, chicken, beef. This helped him keep muscle while losing fat. Added Dark Berries: Two cups a day of blueberries, blackberries, or raspberries. These are loaded with polyphenols that research shows help target visceral fat specifically by reducing inflammation in the gut. Increasing Fiber Intake: Spinach, kale, arugula. We also add psyllium husk and chia seeds. Fiber feeds your good gut bacteria while also helping you feel fuller. Chrono Eating Protocol: We want to align our meal schedule with our circadian rhythms to automate hunger, improve sleep, and digestion. Step 1: The Optimized Mediterranean Diet (OMD) We set the final meal 3 to 5 hours before bed. First meal 1 to 2 hours after waking. One meal in between. This is where Mike was making the same mistake I see constantly. He was doing hours of cardio. Treadmill. Bike. Boot camps. 7 days a week, but his visceral fat kept on going up. Doing more cardio is NOT the answer for visceral fat. It can actually be detrimental due to the increase in cortisol. The best exercises to reduce visceral fat are weight training, walking, and HIIT once a week. Study after study shows that these 3 types of training are ideal for reducing visceral fat. Muscle is a metabolically active tissue, walking burns calories and reduces stress (cortisol), and the occasional HIIT workout triggers large metabolic and hormonal responses. Mike’s new program was simple: Three focused lifting sessions per week Hitting each muscle group at least 2x A HIIT workout once a week was optional He literally spent half the time in the gym and got way better results. For movement, we replaced cardio with what I call mindless movement. Walking meetings. Taking stairs. An under-desk treadmill. The goal was 7,000 to 10,000 steps daily without thinking about it. This is the one Mike resisted longest. He was an “I’ll sleep when I’m dead” guy. Five to six hours a night. Step 2: The VAT Burn Protocol Step 3: The Sundown Switch When you constantly deprive your body of sleep, your cortisol spikes. Insulin sensitivity tanks. Testosterone drops. All while ghrelin, your hunger hormone, increases, leptin, your satiety hormone, decreases. One study found that people sleeping 5.5 hours versus 8.5 hours, eating the same calories, lost 55% less fat. I don’t know about you, but that sounds horrible. We made simple changes: Consistent bedtime and wake time 30 to 60 minute wind-down routine Dim lights in the evening Bluelight blockers on if he goes on screens Cool bedroom (68 to 72 degrees) No alcohol (obviously) With a good routine and automatic protocol, Mike gained an extra hour of sleep while improving the quality of those hours. Everything clicked. His appetite is regulated. His energy improved. His stress dropped. Sleep is the lead domino. Fix that, and a lot of other things fix themselves. If you have a significant amount of visceral fat to lose, 90 days is a realistic timeframe to see major reductions. That’s one quarter of a year. Not long when you consider what’s at stake. Mike took the protocol seriously. In 90 days, he dropped 75% of his visceral fat while also losing 30+ pounds of fat. But we didn’t stop there. We spent a full year together rewiring his behaviors and identity. Now, his visceral fat is near zero. He’s added muscle. He’s at his lowest weight since his freshman year of college. And health has become his 90 Days Is All It Takes number one asset instead of his biggest liability. More energy. Sharper thinking. Better performance at work. More presence with his family. That’s the difference between a protocol and a transformation. The protocol gets you results. The year rewires who you are. When you fix the system, your body starts working with you instead of against you. Onward and upward.  - Dan Disclaimer: This email is provided for educational and informational purposes only and does not constitute providing medical advice or professional services. The information provided should not be used for diagnosing or treating a health problem or disease, and those seeking personal medical advice should consult with a licensed physician. title: Vem ser um Data Creator
author: Desconhecido
summary: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. Vem ser um Data Creator Vagas abertas :) HEITOR SASAKI MAR 10 LEIA NO APP Tu já sabes trabalhar com dados. Sabes rodar query, montar dashboard, apresentar resultado. Isso tu aprendeste há tempo. Só que a tua carreira travou num lugar estr…
fullHtml: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. Vem ser um Data Creator Vagas abertas :) HEITOR SASAKI MAR 10 LEIA NO APP Tu já sabes trabalhar com dados. Sabes rodar query, montar dashboard, apresentar resultado. Isso tu aprendeste há tempo. Só que a tua carreira travou num lugar estranho: tu entregas bem, tu resolves problema, e mesmo assim a promoção não vem. Ou vem, mas pra outra pessoa. Ou tu olhas pro mercado e parece que todo mundo tá no mesmo nível que tu — e aí fica difícil se destacar. O problema raramente é técnico. O problema é que tu tá cercado das pessoas erradas, falando sobre os assuntos errados, e contando a história errada sobre ti. Uma comunidade de 200+ profissionais de dados — analistas, cientistas, engenheiros — que se ajudam mutuamente a crescer na carreira. Data Creators O que é o Data Creators Mas “comunidade” virou uma palavra vazia. Então deixa eu te mostrar o que isso significa na prática. Indicações reais de vagas, toda semana. Eu procuro vagas ativamente e posto no grupo. Em média, são 2-3 indicações por semana. Quando um DC muda de empresa e precisa contratar, ele indica outro DC. Quando um recrutador manda mensagem pra um de nós, a vaga circula no grupo antes de ir pro LinkedIn. Isso funciona porque o grupo foi ficando cada vez mais sênior. Temos gente na 3M, Itaú, Nubank, iFood, XP, Nestlé, J&J, QuintoAndar, Boticário, Kenvue, Raizen. Gente na Holanda, Alemanha, EUA, Canadá. Quando esse pessoal se movimenta, as oportunidades se movimentam junto. 80% das vagas boas nunca aparecem no LinkedIn. Elas circulam por indicação. A pergunta é se tu estás na rede onde elas circulam. Reuniões mensais sobre o que realmente separa pleno de sênior. Aqui é onde o DC é diferente de qualquer curso ou mentoria que tu já viste. Trago experts pra falar sobre os assuntos que ninguém ensina mas que todo gestor cobra: pricing, engenharia de dados (o pensamento por trás da solução, não a ferramenta), gestão de mudanças, governança de dados, como influenciar decisão sem ter autoridade formal. Tu já percebeste que o analista que sobe pra sênior raramente é o mais técnico da sala. É o que entende o negócio, sabe comunicar impacto, e consegue fazer as coisas acontecerem dentro de uma empresa que resiste a mudança. Essas reuniões existem pra te dar o repertório que faz essa diferença. Reposicionamento profissional com prompts de IA. Desenvolvi prompts específicos pra cada etapa: otimizar teu LinkedIn, refinar teu CV pra passar nos ATS, preparar pra entrevista, encontrar os O que acontece dentro do DC requerimentos fantasmas, traduzir tua experiência pra o mercado internacional e escrever na rede. Cada prompt tem mais de 20 páginas. Eles fazem o trabalho que eu faria se sentasse contigo 1-1: extraem o que tu já tens de bom e transformam numa narrativa que comunica senioridade. Grupo de WhatsApp ativo todo dia. Tu estás em dúvida se aceita uma proposta. Se muda de empresa. Se pede aumento. Se vale a pena fazer aquela pós. Se o teu gestor tá sendo razoável ou se tu tás engolindo sapo. Tu mandas no grupo. Em 20 minutos, tem 3-4 pessoas com experiência real te dando perspectiva. Gente que já passou por isso. Gente que trabalha em empresa parecida com a tua. Eu tô lá todo dia. Os outros DCs também. Não é grupo morto. É onde a galera troca experiência sobre problema real do trabalho — o tipo de conversa que tu não tem com colega de empresa porque tem política no meio. O padrão que eu vejo se repetir é mais ou menos assim: Nos primeiros 30 dias, o DC absorve. Entra no grupo, lê as conversas, assiste as calls gravadas. Começa a perceber que tem gente com problemas parecidos e que as soluções já existem — só estavam em outro lugar. Entre 2 e 3 meses, começa a agir. Refaz o CV, muda o LinkedIn, começa a se posicionar diferente em entrevista. As indicações de vaga começam a fazer sentido porque agora ele sabe o que procurar. Depois de 6 meses, o DC que entrou inseguro sobre o próprio nível já tá ajudando os mais novos. Já indicou gente pra vaga, já participou de reunião que mudou como ele pensa sobre a própria área. Alguns resultados concretos: O caminho de quem já tá dentro Um DC entrou como júnior, participou de todas as calls, usou os prompts pra se reposicionar. Hoje é especialista. Levou 2 anos. Um DC veio da física, sem experiência formal em dados. Usou a rede do grupo pra encontrar a primeira oportunidade. Hoje trabalha como cientista de dados. Um DC já trocou de empresa três vezes desde que entrou. Virou piada interna. Toda vez que ele aparece no grupo dizendo “pessoal, tenho uma novidade...” a galera já sabe. Não vou te prometer resultado específico porque isso depende de ti. O que eu prometo é o ambiente, as ferramentas e as pessoas certas. O resto é contigo. Tu já trabalhas com dados (ou em outra área).. Tu sabes o básico. O que tu precisas agora é de: Uma rede de contatos que gera oportunidades reais (não conexões vazias no LinkedIn) Repertório de negócio que te posiciona como sênior (pricing, governança, gestão de mudanças, engenharia de dados) Alguém que te ajude a contar a tua história profissional de um jeito que comunica o teu real valor Se tu estás procurando curso de SQL ou Python, o DC não é pra ti. Tem material ótimo e gratuito pra isso na internet. Se tu tás procurando o empurrão que falta pra dar o próximo passo — seja promoção, seja nova vaga, seja trabalhar pra fora — aí sim. Acesso vitalício à comunidade Data Creators (Whatsapp) Indicações de vagas toda semana (eu procuro ativamente) Pra quem é o Data Creators O que tu recebes Reuniões mensais com experts sobre negócio, governança, pricing, eng. de dados Prompts de IA pra LinkedIn, CV, entrevista e reposicionamento internacional Call inicial comigo pra alinhar onde tu estás e onde queres chegar Biblioteca de conteúdo: calls gravadas, templates, materiais de todas as reuniões anteriores Acesso a uma rede de profissionais em 3M, Itaú, Nubank, iFood, XP, Nestlé, J&J e dezenas de outras empresas R$ 2.500 no Pix. Pagamento único, acesso vitalício. No cartão, a partir de R$ 2.700. Pra dar perspectiva: uma única indicação que te coloque numa vaga que paga R$2.000 a mais por mês já pagou o investimento no primeiro mês. E tu vais ter acesso a indicações toda semana. 7 dias. Se não gostares, peço desculpa e devolvo teu dinheiro. Mas não vais poder voltar por 1 ano. Teus resultados dependem das tuas habilidades e do teu comprometimento. Não tenho como prometer que tu vais conseguir vaga X em Y meses. O que eu faço: te mostro o que eu faria no teu lugar, te conecto com gente que já fez o caminho, e te dou as ferramentas pra encurtar o processo. Isso é o máximo que posso prometer dentro dos limites éticos. E é o que eu prometo. Investimento Garantia Sem garantias de resultado Me manda mensagem e confirma teu interesse. Fazemos a inscrição, te dou as boas vindas, e marcamos uma call pra traçar um plano pro teu caso. Para ver os resultados do DC, cheque o IG: instagram.com/sasakiheitor Siga nas redes: Instagram (Updates do Data Creator, Caixinha de Perguntas) COMPARTILHAR CURTIRCOMENTARRESTACK © 2026 Heitor Sasaki 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 Cancelar inscrição Como entrar

author: Desconhecido
summary: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. Requerimentos Fantasmas HEITOR SASAKI MAR 8 LEIA NO APP Foto de Tandem X Visuals na Unsplash Eu já fiz muitas entrevistas. Muitas mesmo. Passei em vagas no Reino Unido, Suécia, Alemanha, França. E ao longo dos anos também ajudei dezenas d…
fullHtml: Encaminhou este email? Inscreva-se aqui para receber mais. Requerimentos Fantasmas HEITOR SASAKI MAR 8 LEIA NO APP Foto de Tandem X Visuals na Unsplash Eu já fiz muitas entrevistas. Muitas mesmo. Passei em vagas no Reino Unido, Suécia, Alemanha, França. E ao longo dos anos também ajudei dezenas de pessoas no Data Creators a se prepararem pra processos seletivos em tudo que é tipo de empresa. Com o tempo, a gente vai percebendo como a banda toca. Tinha gente que chegava na entrevista preparada. Respondia tudo certo. Demonstrava domínio técnico. SQL, Python, storytelling, caso de negócio — tudo redondinho. E mesmo assim perdia a vaga pra alguém com menos experiência. Durante anos achei que era azar. Viés. Política interna. Até que comecei a conversar com hiring managers e líderes de dados o suficiente pra entender o que estava realmente acontecendo. Todo time de dados carrega um problema que nunca aparece na descrição da vaga. Ele existe na cabeça do gestor, nas conversas de corredor, nas frustrações que acumularam até alguém decidir abrir aquela requisição. Eu chamo de requerimento fantasma. É o critério real de contratação. O que define quem fica com a vaga. E ele quase nunca está escrito em lugar nenhum. O RH recebe uma dor complexa do time e traduz num parágrafo genérico de requisitos. Filtra o que é “apresentável” e publica. Metade do contexto se perde nessa tradução. O requerimento fantasma mora nessa metade perdida. Se tu acompanhas essa humilde newsletter sabe que os problemas de times de dados se repetem com uma consistência impressionante. Quando tu conheces esses padrões, tu começas a ler as entrelinhas de qualquer vaga. Tu chegas na entrevista e tocas no ponto que o gestor está perdendo o sono por causa. Quando isso acontece, ele relaxa. Sente que tu já entendes o que está acontecendo ali dentro. E a entrevista vira uma conversa entre colegas. As entrevistas em que eu me dei melhor na vida tinham algo em comum, eu consegui investigar o que realmente estava acontecendo antes de sair respondendo. (inclusive esse entendimento veio depois de estudar sobre marketing/ vendas, tudo está conectado..) No Data Creators, a gente construiu um prompt que faz isso de forma estruturada. Tu alimentas com a descrição da vaga e o teu perfil, ele identifica os possíveis requerimentos fantasmas, cruza padrões do setor e do cargo, e te dá perguntas específicas pra investigar na entrevista se eles realmente existem. Mas o prompt é só uma ferramenta. O que importa é a mudança de perspectiva. A descrição da vaga é um rascunho público de uma necessidade privada. Quando tu entendes isso, tu começas a ler vagas de um jeito completamente diferente. Heitor Sasaki O primeiro mês na firma foi excelente e estou me saindo melhor do que eu pensava. Quem diria que estudar/escrever toda semana me ajudaria né..? Por isso, to com 5 vagas abertas para o DC. Se queres participar, leia o doc e me mande mensagem no Linkedin/insta. Vais ter acesso a todos os nossos materiais + os prompts que temos (escrita, entrevista, currículo, e o DVP) (podes ver os resultados do DC no Insta) Quero ser um DC (clique) Siga nas redes: Instagram (Updates do Data Creator, Caixinha de Perguntas) COMPARTILHAR CURTIRCOMENTARRESTACK © 2026 Heitor Sasaki 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 Cancelar inscrição Data Creators

title: 4 Minute Fridays The Best Exercises To Lose Visceral Fat, The 3 Hour Rule For Better Heart Health & A Book On A N…
author: Dan Go
summary: 4 Minute Fridays: The Best Exercises To Lose Visceral Fat, The 3-Hour Rule For Better Heart Health & A Book On A N... 4 Minute Fridays: The Best Exercises To Lose Visceral Fat, The 3-Hour Rule For Better Heart Health & A Book On A New Life Changing Therapy This article first appeared in my privat…
fullHtml: 4 Minute Fridays: The Best Exercises To Lose Visceral Fat, The 3-Hour Rule For Better Heart Health & A Book On A N... 4 Minute Fridays: The Best Exercises To Lose Visceral Fat, The 3-Hour Rule For Better Heart Health & A Book On A New Life Changing Therapy This article first appeared in my private newsletter on February DAN GO MAR 6 READ IN APP First Posted on Dango.co Welcome to 4 Minute Fridays, my weekly newsletter, where I reveal cutting-edge tips to help you get lean, boost energy, and live longer. The best exercises to lose visceral fat The 3-hour rule for better heart health A book on a life-changing therapy What’s In Store For Today: Quick correction: Tuesday's email said waist-to-hip ratio when I meant waist-to-height. My brain apparently skipped leg day. Thanks to those who emailed me to point it out. Appreciate you. Hey friend, Writing to you from Mexico, near Puerto Vallarta, where things have been ... interesting. If you’ve been following the news, you know something went down here this week. Streets shut down, flights were grounded, and chaos seemed imminent. Thankfully, after about 24 hours, things returned to normal and, for the most part, we were safe the whole time. Amidst all the confusion, something stood out to me. Being on the ground while social media reported the opposite was eye- opening. What locals were experiencing and what was being posted online were two completely different realities. After the first day, civilians felt safe. Tourists went about their day. Yet the feeds were full of panic and outrage designed for clicks, not truth. This was my first front-row seat to disinformation in real time. Something this taught me was that not everything you read is real, especially online. Stay skeptical. Find better sources. Thankfully, we’re headed back. Can’t wait to send the next 4MF to you from the comfort of my home. Here Is Your 4 Minute Friday: 1. The Best Exercises To Burn Visceral Fat Most people think cardio is the answer to belly fat. It’s not. Visceral fat is the dangerous fat wrapped around your organs. You can’t see it, but it’s linked to heart disease, diabetes, and cognitive decline. And daily cardio can actually make it worse by spiking cortisol. Here’s what the research actually shows: A 2025 review found that strength training significantly reduces visceral fat by building muscle. This is because muscle acts as a metabolic sink. More muscle means your body pulls more glucose and fatty acids out of your blood, even at rest. Walking beats running for visceral fat. Moderate-intensity walking lowers cortisol while burning fat preferentially. Extensive cardio spikes stress hormones, which can actually promote visceral fat storage, especially if you’re already stressed. Sprinting is one of the most effective tools we have. It triggers the release of epinephrine, norepinephrine, and growth hormone, which specifically target visceral fat stores. A 2024 study found that just one HIIT session per week for 8 weeks significantly reduced belly fat and waist circumference. For our clients, we do strength training 3 times a week, making sure we’re hitting up each muscle group 2x a week. We walk every single day using a protocol we call “mindless movement”. Sprinting is optional. We like to add these later on into a client’s program, just due to the intensity it would have on their joints. A new study from Northwestern just proved something I’ve been telling clients for years: stop eating 3 hours before bed. 2. The 3-Hour Rule For Better Heart Health Researchers put 39 overweight adults through a simple test. One group stopped eating at least 3 hours before sleep and extended their overnight fast to 13-16 hours. The control group ate normally. Without changing what or how much they ate, the group that stopped eating 3 hours before bed saw real improvements. Overnight diastolic blood pressure dipping improved by 3.5%. Overnight, heart rate dropped by 5%. They also had higher HRV, lower cortisol, and better insulin sensitivity the next day. This works because sleep is when your body regulates cardiovascular and metabolic function. Eating too close to bed disrupts that process. Your body can’t fully shift into recovery mode when it’s still digesting. The best part? 90% of participants stuck with it. It’s not a dramatic diet overhaul. Just close the kitchen earlier. What you eat matters. But so does when. I don’t talk about this much, but psychedelic therapy was one of the most life-changing experiences of my life. I’ll get deeper into my personal story in a future newsletter, but for now, I want to point you to this book. Psychedelic Therapy was written by friends of mine who have been at the forefront of the research and real-world application of this work. It’s a thoughtful, grounded exploration of how psychedelic therapy can support deeper healing, address trauma at the root, and expand what’s possible in mental health care. Whether you’re just beginning to explore this topic or already engaged with it, this is a great place to start. And until midnight tonight, pre-ordering the hardcover gets you complimentary access to a live online summit featuring Gábor Maté, ❤️ My Favourite Thing This Week Michael Pollan, Melissa Etheridge, and other leading voices in this space. If you’re curious about how psychedelic therapy supports mental health, brain resilience, and long-term wellbeing, grab the book and unlock your bonus summit access before midnight tonight. Mark is an executive who wanted more than just weight loss, he wanted clarity, energy, and long-term sustainability. He’s now down 26.98 lbs, lost 5.62 inches off his waist, and is sleeping better, feeling better, and thinking more clearly than he has in years. What surprised him most was how sustainable the program felt, and once he focused on consistency and total progress (inches lost, better blood pressure, improved bloodwork) instead of just the scale, every metric started moving in the right direction. Here’s how we did it: Built simple, repeatable nutrition habits Measured success beyond just body weight Prioritized strength training and recovery Mark didn’t just drop pounds, he upgraded his entire system. Excellent work, Mark! Are you an entrepreneur who wants to drop the fat, gain muscle, and boost energy? Click this link to apply for our coaching program. Client Of The Week - Mark, Executive “A fool pulls the leaves. A brute chops the trunk. A sage digs the roots.” ― Pierce Brown from the book Golden Son When a problem arises, I always remind myself to never fix the symptom and to always focus on the causes. The symptoms are the leaves, the causes are the roots. As promised, get healthier in under 4 minutes. - Dan PS: Of all the shells I own, this one is my favorite. Chen X, et al. “Effects of various exercise types on visceral adipose tissue in individuals with overweight and obesity: A systematic review and network meta-analysis of 84 randomized controlled trials.” Obesity Reviews. 2024 Mar;25(3):e13666. Jayedi A, et al. “Aerobic Exercise and Weight Loss in Adults: A Systematic Review and Dose-Response Meta-Analysis.” JAMA Network Open. 2024 Dec 2;7(12):e2452185. Poon ET-C, et al. “Efficacy of Interval Training in Improving Body Composition and Adiposity in Apparently Healthy Adults: An Umbrella Review with Meta-Analysis.” Sports Medicine. 2024 Jul; Zhu X, et al. “The release of lipolytic hormones during various high-intensity interval and moderate-intensity continuous training regimens and their effects on fat loss.” Journal of Sports Science and Medicine. 2024;23:559-570. Grimaldi D, Reid KJ, Abbott SM, Knutson KL, Zee PC. “Sleep-Aligned Extended Overnight Fasting Improves Nighttime and Daytime Cardiometabolic Function.” Arteriosclerosis, Thrombosis, and Vascular Biology. 2026.
